mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are negative on average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate held below 0.25 percent suppresses short-term interest rates and risk-free returns, pushing portfolio managers into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad contraction in final demand that spills into manufacturing output, triggering downward earnings revisions that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 7 percent, a rising unemployment rate intensifies household balance sheet stress and compresses discretionary spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 60 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent and increases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing activity and improving operating leverage, which lifts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative on average over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a distressed residential construction sector and weak household formation, which drags aggregate demand lower and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST is below 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent and an unemployment rate above 8 percent defines a liquidity trap with elevated slack; the absence of yield alternatives and anticipated policy backstop drive capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with high unemployment
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent and UNRATE is above 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real chained GDP signals the pace of aggregate demand expansion; positive growth drives upward revisions to corporate sales expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 increases are followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.
